title: rosa sepium subsp. klukii
intro: Rosa sepium subsp. klukii, also known as the Rosa Klukii or wild rose, is a subspecies of the Rosa sepium species, a perennial shrub native to Eastern Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Rosa sepium subsp. klukii include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable herbal remedy. Rosa sepium subsp. klukii can be prepared as a tea, decoction, or tincture, allowing for various herbal preparations to be used in traditional medicine.
